title: aconitum lycoctonum subsp. penninum
intro: Aconitum lycoctonum subsp. penninum, also known as Monk's Hood or Wolf's Bane, is a subspecies of the Aconitum lycoctonum plant, a perennial flowering plant in the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which have been found to have various health benefits, including pain relief, reduced inflammation, and improved cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the root and rhizome of Aconitum lycoctonum subsp. penninum are typically dried and used to create tinctures, decoctions, and infusions, although caution is advised due to its toxicity. However, its medicinal properties and health benefits make it a valuable plant in some traditional medicine practices, but it should only be used under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
